titleStmt.title: Letter from A.J. Cockington to the Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 24 October 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: A.J. Cockington

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from A.J. Cockington, on behalf of the General Council of Medical Education & Registration for the United Kingdom, to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) regarding the application of Ulick Joseph Burke to be reinstated on the medical register. Cockington enquiries whether there would be any objection to Burke's reinstatement.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Journal pp 300 & 306 General Council of
Medical Education & Registration
of the United Kingdom,
299, Oxford Street, London, W. October 24, 1916. Dear Sir, Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the General
Medical Council, I have to inform you that the name of
Mick Joseph Burke
was erased from the Medical Register on November 3, 1915
that at the time he held the qualification of L.L.M.R.C.P. Irel. 1896
that he has made an application for the restoration of his
name; and that this application will be considered by the
executive committee of the Council on November 27, 1916. I am directed to inquire whether (a) he still
holds any qualification granted by your Body that entitles
him to be registered, and (b) whether you know of any
objection to the restoration of his name. I am,
Yours faithfully, AJ Cockington,
Acting Registrar. The Registrar of the
Royal College of Physicians
of Ireland. J.V.C. 3/11/16
